SSU-Centric Topology Analysis Dashboard

Topology figures are shown first for direct visual comparison. Each figure uses an explicit layered layout: SSUs at the bottom row, Unions above them, and topology-level structure arranged in deterministic grids instead of force-directed placement.

2D-FullMesh

4 x 4 exchange nodes | 16 exchange nodes
Click a node to highlight one-hop neighbors, incident links, and link bandwidth

Topology Figure

SSUs stay on the bottom row and Unions sit on the layer above inside each exchange node. Exchange nodes are arranged on a structured horizontal and vertical grid.

Key Metrics

Diameter4
Average Hops3.51
Bisection Bandwidth400.0 Gbps
Backend Links96

Default Route Throughput

A2A Per SSU Throughput and sparse per-SSU throughput are shown for the default route only.
DOR ThroughputA2A 189.55 Gbps
DOR ThroughputSparse 200.00 Gbps

Routing Mode

Active ModeSHORTEST_PATH
Backend PatternEach Union uses 6 backend ports: 3 row links on the X axis and 3 column links on the Y axis, so both Union chips carry the same 4x4 full-mesh structure.
Message Size4.00 MB
Sparse Active Ratio25%
Sparse Target Count2
A2A Scopeall SSUs send to every other SSU

Routing Diversity Snapshot

SHORTEST_PATH vs DOR: 1.60x average path diversity (3.20 vs 2.00 end-to-end paths per inter-exchange SSU pair); FULL_PATH reaches 12.00 on average with peak 12 paths.
DOR Avg 2.00 | Peak 2
SHORTEST_PATH Avg 3.20 | Peak 4
FULL_PATH Avg 12.00 | Peak 12

A2A Routing Comparison

Mode Per SSU Throughput Completion Time P95 Completion Max Link Utilization Link Utilization CV
DOR 189.55 Gbps 0.0214 s 0.0107 s 95.52% 0.000
SHORTEST_PATH 189.55 Gbps 0.0214 s 0.0107 s 95.52% 0.000
FULL_PATH 95.25 Gbps 0.0427 s 0.0107 s 100.00% 0.153

Sparse 1-to-N Routing Comparison

Mode Per SSU Throughput Completion Time P95 Completion Max Link Utilization Link Utilization CV
DOR 200.00 Gbps 0.0003 s 0.0002 s 75.00% 0.781
SHORTEST_PATH 200.00 Gbps 0.0003 s 0.0002 s 56.25% 0.487
FULL_PATH 200.00 Gbps 0.0003 s 0.0002 s 72.92% 0.246

Analysis Notes

  • same-exchange SSU traffic stays inside the exchange node via Union switching
  • source SSU traffic evenly splits across both local 200 Gbps uplinks into the two Union planes
  • inter-exchange SSU traffic is modeled as source SSU -> source Union -> backend topology -> destination Union -> destination SSU
  • destination-side traffic evenly splits from the two destination Unions down to the target SSU
  • both Union chips expose the same direct-connect backend plane, so routing decisions are evaluated independently on each Union plane
  • SHORTEST_PATH splits each Union plane across all shortest Union-to-Union paths without a fixed dimension order
  • A2A per-SSU throughput reaches 189.55 Gbps with maximum backend link utilization 95.52%.
  • Sparse 1-to-N communication completes in 0.24 ms at p95 with link-utilization CV 0.487.
  • Structural span is diameter 4 and average hops 3.51.

Routing Mode

Active ModeSHORTEST_PATH

Sparse Active Ratio25%

Analysis Notes

  • same-exchange SSU traffic stays inside the exchange node via Union switching
  • source SSU traffic evenly splits across both local 200 Gbps uplinks into the two Union planes
  • inter-exchange SSU traffic is modeled as source SSU -> source Union -> backend topology -> destination Union -> destination SSU
  • destination-side traffic evenly splits from the two destination Unions down to the target SSU
  • both Union chips expose the same direct-connect backend plane, so routing decisions are evaluated independently on each Union plane
  • SHORTEST_PATH splits each Union plane across all shortest Union-to-Union paths without a fixed dimension order
  • A2A per-SSU throughput reaches 99.22 Gbps with maximum backend link utilization 100.00%.
  • Sparse 1-to-N communication completes in 0.24 ms at p95 with link-utilization CV 0.291.

Analysis Notes

  • same-exchange SSU traffic stays inside the exchange node via Union switching
  • source SSU traffic evenly splits across both local 200 Gbps uplinks into the two Union planes
  • inter-exchange SSU traffic is modeled as source SSU -> source Union -> backend topology -> destination Union -> destination SSU
  • destination-side traffic evenly splits from the two destination Unions down to the target SSU
  • both Union chips expose the same direct-connect backend plane, so routing decisions are evaluated independently on each Union plane
  • SHORTEST_PATH splits each Union plane across all shortest Union-to-Union paths without a fixed dimension order
  • A2A per-SSU throughput reaches 99.80 Gbps with maximum backend link utilization 100.00%.

Clos

18 exchange nodes | 18 exchange nodes
Click a node to highlight one-hop neighbors, incident links, and link bandwidth

Topology Figure

SSUs stay on the bottom row and Unions sit on the layer above inside each exchange node. Clos spine layer sits above the exchange-node Union layer for structured two-level viewing.

Key Metrics

Diameter4
Average Hops3.90
Bisection Bandwidth400.0 Gbps
Backend Links144

Default Route Throughput

A2A Per SSU Throughput and sparse per-SSU throughput are shown for the default route only.
ECMP ThroughputA2A 200.00 Gbps
ECMP ThroughputSparse 200.00 Gbps

Routing Mode

Active ModeECMP
Backend PatternEach exchange node keeps two independent Union planes; each Union uplinks via 4 x 400 Gbps into its own Clos spine pool, for 8 x 400 Gbps total per exchange node.
Message Size4.00 MB
Sparse Active Ratio25%
Sparse Target Count2
A2A Scopeall SSUs send to every other SSU

ECMP Workload Summary

Workload Per SSU Throughput Completion Time P95 Completion Max Link Utilization Link Utilization CV
A2A 200.00 Gbps 0.0229 s 0.0114 s 95.10% 0.000
Sparse 1-to-N 200.00 Gbps 0.0003 s 0.0002 s 40.62% 0.408

Analysis Notes

  • same-exchange SSU traffic stays inside the exchange node via Union switching
  • source SSU traffic evenly splits across both local 200 Gbps uplinks into the two Union planes
  • inter-exchange SSU traffic is modeled as source SSU -> source Union -> backend topology -> destination Union -> destination SSU
  • destination-side traffic evenly splits from the two destination Unions down to the target SSU
  • ECMP splits Clos traffic across equal-cost shortest paths through the upper Union stage
  • each exchange contributes 4 x 400 Gbps from union0 and 4 x 400 Gbps from union1 into separate Clos spine pools
  • A2A per-SSU throughput reaches 200.00 Gbps with maximum backend link utilization 95.10%.
  • Sparse 1-to-N communication completes in 0.16 ms at p95 with link-utilization CV 0.408.
  • Structural span is diameter 4 and average hops 3.90.
